description: Run the package-local TypeScript validation command using the repository package manager and scripts.
body: Inputs: changed files, package directory, package.json, lockfile, tsconfig, and workspace layout. Procedure: identify the owning package, inspect scripts for typecheck/build/test/codegen ordering, choose bun/npm/pnpm/yarn according to lockfile or project convention, and run the narrowest package-local command that validates types. Do not invoke raw tsc directly when scripts wrap generation, project references, env setup, or framework compilers. Output: command, cwd, package manager, exit code, and first actionable diagnostic or pass summary. Failure handling: distinguish compiler errors from missing dependency, wrong cwd, script absence, or root guard failures. Use when TypeScript / JavaScript work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: run the documented inspection and validation workflow; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
